# Tokenomics

> The final POLAR allocation, vesting schedule and launch parameters will be published closer to launch once they have been finalized.

Canonical URL: https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/architecture/tokenomics
Markdown URL: https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/architecture/tokenomics.md

---

The final POLAR allocation, vesting schedule and launch parameters will be published closer to launch once they have been finalized.

This section focuses on the economic principles behind POLAR.

## Supply

POLAR will launch with an initial supply of 100 million tokens, distributed across the treasury, community, team and investors. Those tokens will gradually enter circulation according to their respective vesting schedules, while the long-term growth of the supply is entirely driven by demand through the conversion mechanism.

As explained in the dedicated section, every new POLAR outside the vesting schedule requires pETH to be permanently converted before it can enter circulation. This ensures that the supply grows alongside the activity taking place across Polaris, keeping the utility token economically connected to the reserve asset supporting the ecosystem.

## A Closed Economy

Polaris is designed as a closed economic system where the value generated by protocol activity remains inside the ecosystem.

Instead of relying on continuous token emissions to attract liquidity, value generated through the core protocol mechanisms is redistributed across the ecosystem according to the role of each asset. Together, they remain connected through the same underlying value flows.

The protocol itself follows the same economic model. Treasury-held POLAR participates through vePOLAR alongside every other long-term holder, aligning incentives across the ecosystem while allowing protocol-generated value to remain inside Polaris.

## vePOLAR

Long-term participation in Polaris takes place through vePOLAR.

Users commit POLAR for a chosen period of time to align themselves with the long-term growth of the ecosystem. In return, they receive a share of protocol-native value, including interest from deployed pAssets, together with stewardship rights. These returns are distributed through protocol activity itself, without relying on additional POLAR incentives.
